definitions: ["Literally 'lacking work': having insufficient work or not enough tasks to occupy oneself."]
["A colloquial term for a destructive fire or blaze."]
["A person who pretends to be tough or brave but is actually weak and easily frightened."]
["Literally, having a cutting edge that is sharp on the lower side and blunt on the upper side."]
idiom: tajam ke bawah, tumpul ke atas

definitions: ["The criminal or unlawful appropriation of funds or property entrusted to someone; embezzlement."]
["A literal phrase meaning 'there is a shrimp behind the rock.'"]
["A casual sexual relationship with no expectation of commitment or continuation; a one-night stand."]
["literal translation: 'banana republic.'"]
["To bring an issue to an end or to consider a matter concluded; to stop further discussion, investigation, or action on a topic."]
["A literal proverb meaning 'calm (or still) water carries away,' describing the behavior of water that appears calm on the surface."]
["Colloquial clipped form of 'bodo amat' used to express indifference; 'I don't care'."]
["A proverb meaning 'still waters run deep,' used to describe a quiet or reserved person who nonetheless has hidden depth, capability, or potential influence."]
["Literally, 'according to you'; a direct phrasing asking for or attributing an opinion to the addressee."]
["To leave something as it is; not to touch, change, or interfere with it."]
["Figuratively, a sarcastic or rhetorical response to an obvious statement meaning 'you think?' or 'you don't say?', used to express surprise-mocking or to highlight the obviousness of something."]
["Literally 'to eat wind': to take in or consume wind or air (literal meaning, rarely used)."]
["Literally, 'the valiant red one'; a personified expression referring to something red or splendid in hue."]
["Literally 'one-night love'; a romantic or sexual encounter that lasts only one night."]
["Literally, 'already fallen, then struck by a ladder'; an image of falling and then being hit by a ladder."]
["Used as an imperative or response meaning 'let it be', 'never mind', 'don't worry about it', or 'leave him/her/them alone'."]
["Literally, 'to drink water while diving'; to perform the action of drinking while one is diving."]
["A literal question meaning 'is there anything?' or 'is something the matter?' used to ask whether something exists or is wrong."]
["People who lack substance, knowledge, or ability often make the most noise or boast the most; empty vessels make the most sound."]
["Literally, 'time of being ignorant' or 'a period of not knowing'; a direct compositional reading of the words rather than a common literal referent."]
["Expresses indifference or apathy; 'I don't care' or 'whatever'."]
["figurative: a person one adores or cherishes; a beloved, darling, or romantic idol."]
["literal translation: 'light-handed' or 'light of hand.'"]
["A substance, such as a spray or coil, used to repel or kill mosquitoes and other insects."]
["A person who steals or habitually takes things that do not belong to them; someone with 'sticky fingers.'"]
["literal translation: 'the heart's adored one.'"]
["Literally, 'to receive an upturned/overturned banana'; a concrete image referring to an easily obtained object."]
["Rice that has been cooked until it has become porridge."]
["To cover or close something physically, often intensively or repeatedly."]
["Used derogatorily to say someone has nothing better to do, implying they waste time on trivial, bothersome, or unnecessary activities."]
["Literally 'very stupid' (from 'bodoh amat')."]
["To suffer an additional misfortune on top of an existing one; to experience multiple setbacks in succession."]
["Literally, hot or molten lead (metal heated until liquid)."]
["Literally, 'three go in, four come out'; a compositional image describing a mismatch between input and output."]
["To go out for a leisurely walk or outing to enjoy fresh air and relaxation."]
["The act or process of making something darker; darkening."]
["To be inactive, dormant, or not functioning (often used figuratively of objects, systems, or organizations)."]
["Literal: to become a person or to attain the status of being a human individual."]
["Literally \"cowboy worm\": a compound of 'koboi' (cowboy) and 'cacing' (worm), a literal phrase with no common neutral usage."]
["Literally, 'time ignorant'; a literal composition of the words but not commonly used on its own to convey a concrete event or object."]
["To close the books; to cease recording transactions and finalize accounting records, typically at the end of a period."]
["Literal: to dig a hole and then fill or close it, referring to the physical action of making and covering an excavation."]
["Literal: plural of kaleng; cans or tins used to hold food or other substances."]
["A person who acts tough or boastful but is actually timid, whiny, or easily upset."]
["To struggle or fight to the utmost limit; to persist to the very last degree."]
["figurative: a derogatory term for a country perceived as politically unstable, corrupt, or governed by dictatorial elites and dependent on a single export commodity."]
["A description of double standards in which rules or laws are applied harshly to the less powerful and leniently to the powerful; one law for the poor and another for the rich."]

definitions: ["Literal: a black goat, referring to an animal of that color."]
["literal translation: 'the favored shoot, and the dish arrives.'"]
["To close a physical book by shutting its covers."]
["Having physically long arms or an extended reach."]
["A third, unwanted person accompanying a pair (a 'third wheel') who disrupts or intrudes on a couple's interaction."]
["Literally 'a durian falling'—an occurrence of durian fruit falling from a tree."]
["To sleep; to rest in a state of reduced consciousness and diminished responsiveness to external stimuli."]
["figurative: disposed to help others readily; quick to lend a hand."]
["Figuratively, to spend more than one earns or to have expenses exceed income; to be in deficit (similar to 'besar pasak daripada tiang')."]
["used as an intensifier in negative or restrictive contexts meaning 'at all' or 'in any way.'"]
["To eat; to consume food."]
["Literal: special requirements or specific needs that differ from the usual or standard."]
["An expression of concern or request for explanation meaning 'what's going on?' or 'what's the matter?' used to prompt someone to explain a situation or their feelings."]
["Idiomatic: specific needs for additional care, services, or accommodations due to disabilities, developmental differences, or other conditions that require specialized support."]
["Colloquial term for bullets or ammunition; projectiles fired from a firearm."]
["A euphemism for having sexual intercourse."]
["To sleep together or share a bed with someone; a literal description of occupying the same bed for rest or sleep."]
["What is done is done; an irreversible situation that cannot be changed."]
["Figurative (social success): to become successful, prominent, or respected; to 'be somebody' in terms of social status or achievement."]
["figurative: finally obtaining something that has been long desired; a wished-for thing or opportunity comes at last."]
["Figurative (maturational): to grow up, mature, or behave responsibly as an adult."]
["Literally, 'until the blood is exhausted'; to continue until one's physical resources are spent."]
["Figurative: not genuine or of poor quality; makeshift, flimsy, or a sham rather than a real or proper item or arrangement."]
["To idle or loiter; to pass time aimlessly by sitting around or wandering without purpose."]
["Figuratively, careless, indifferent, or apathetic; used to indicate that one does not care about someone or something."]
["figurative: quick to strike or prone to hitting others; given to physical aggression."]
["Figuratively, to gain a profit, benefit, or windfall effortlessly or without hard work; to receive an unexpected or easy reward."]
["literal translation: 'entirely' or 'completely.'"]
["An idiom meaning one's expenditures exceed one's income; spending more than one can afford."]
["An unexpected windfall or stroke of good fortune; a sudden profitable gain."]
["A hidden or internal enemy; a person within a group who secretly betrays or works against that group."]
["To accomplish two aims in a single action; to obtain an additional benefit while carrying out another task."]
["An enemy physically concealed under bedding or inside a blanket."]
["To know one's place; to behave with appropriate modesty or restraint and avoid presumptuous conduct."]
["To conceal, hide, or disguise information, facts, or wrongdoing; to cover up."]
["Figurative: a person or group unfairly blamed for the faults, mistakes, or problems of others; a scapegoat."]
["An idiom meaning there is a concealed ulterior motive or hidden catch behind an action, offer, or statement."]
["Literally, an empty barrel makes a loud noise."]
["Literally \"cowboy crybaby\": a compound of 'koboi' (cowboy) and 'cengeng' (crying or whiny person), a literal combination used mainly for effect."]
["A euphemistic idiom meaning to have sexual intercourse with someone."]
["To know oneself; to understand one's own situation, abilities, or identity."]
["Figuratively, an expression of indifference or lack of concern; equivalent to 'I don't care' or 'whatever'."]
["Figurative: to borrow or take on new obligations in order to pay off existing ones, creating a cycle of debt or temporary fixes that conceal underlying problems."]
["A literal phrase meaning 'the peg (stake) is larger than the post,' picturing an imbalance between components of a structure."]
